title: Seniors to cop a $1600 health cover hike
full_text: Other Editions 05 May 2026 News Seniors to cop a $1600 health cover hike Daily Telegraph Julie Cross 5 May 2026 279 words English DAITEL 5 © News Pty Limited. No redistribution is permitted. Older Australians are facing private health insurance price hikes of more than $1600 from April next year, under the Albanese government’s plan to scrap higher rebates for over-65s. Modelling by Private Healthcare Australia (PHA) found some retirees could see increases three times higher than younger Australians. The peak private insurance body said Australians aged 70 and above with gold hospital cover could see premiums jump by more than 21 per cent in April, compared to about 4 per cent for people under 65. The figures show the typical premium rise for a couple with gold cover could be $1614, based on the average price rises this year, where gold cover increased significantly more than other policies. A single person over 70 could see an $807 jump. Meanwhile, a typical premium rise for a couple over 70 with silver cover could rise by $459, and for a single, $299. PHA chief executive Dr Rachel David said the policy risks placing hospital cover beyond the reach of many retirees living on fixed incomes, including more than 400,000 pensioners. “These are Australians who have done exactly what governments encouraged them to do – maintain health cover throughout their lives so they can access private healthcare and take pressure off the public system,” Dr David said. “Now, just when they need their hospital cover most, they are being singled out for the biggest price increases we’ve ever seen.” Last month, Health Minister Mark Butler shocked many when he announced the government would remove the higher rebate for private health insurance for over-65s. summary: Older Australians will face significant increases in private health insurance premiums, with hikes exceeding $1600 from April 2027 due to the Albanese government's decision to eliminate higher rebates for those over 65. The modelling indicates that retirees, particularly those aged 70 and above, could see premium increases of over 21%, compared to around 4% for younger individuals. This change may impact the affordability of hospital cover for many retirees on fixed incomes.
publication:
Seniors to cop a $1600 health cover hike
Daily Telegraph05 May 2026279 wordsJulie Cross

Older Australians are facing private health insurance price hikes of more than $1600 from April next year, under the Albanese government’s plan to scrap higher rebates for over-65s.
heading: Health and Disability Insurance
key_points: Private health insurance premiums for seniors will rise by more than $1600 from April 2027.
Retirees aged 70 and above could experience premium increases of over 21%.
The removal of higher rebates for over-65s could make hospital cover unaffordable for many retirees.
relevance: This news is relevant to actuaries and financial services professionals as it highlights changes in health insurance policies that could affect risk assessments and financial planning for retirees.
